messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-project",
      "title": "Initialize project scaffold",
      "description": "Create project folder, initialize npm (package.json), and create source directories (src, src/public, src/server, src/styles). Add basic .gitignore and npm scripts placeholders.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A project directory exists with package.json, src/public and src/server directories, a .gitignore, and npm scripts placeholders in package.json."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install Node and Tailwind related dependencies",
      "description": "Install Express and development tools: tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer, and optionally nodemon; add install and start/build scripts to package.json.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "node_modules contains express and tailwind tooling; package.json has scripts for dev (e.g., nodemon), build (tailwind build), and start (node server)."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind CSS build",
      "description": "Create tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js, add Tailwind directives to a src/styles/input.css, and add npm build script to generate a bundled CSS file in src/public (e.g., src/public/styles.css).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js exist; running the build script produces src/public/styles.css containing compiled Tailwind CSS."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-express-server",
      "title": "Implement Express server to serve static UI and API",
      "description": "Create an Express app (src/server/index.js) that serves static files from src/public and exposes REST endpoints GET /api/color and POST /api/color to read and update the current text color.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Express server starts via npm start; requesting GET /api/color returns JSON color value; POST /api/color updates color and returns success status."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-persistence",
      "title": "Add simple persistence for color",
      "description": "Implement a small persistence layer (e.g., a JSON file like data/color.json) or lightweight in-memory fallback so that GET /api/color returns the saved color and POST /api/color writes updates to the JSON file.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Color value persists across server restarts when stored in the JSON file; GET returns the persisted value and POST updates the file."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-frontend-ui",
      "title": "Create frontend page with color controls",
      "description": "Build an HTML page (src/public/index.html) styled with Tailwind showing the 'hello world' text, a color input (color picker), preset color buttons, and client-side JS that fetches GET /api/color on load and calls POST /api/color on changes to update text color live.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-express-server",
        "implement-persistence"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "Opening the site shows 'hello world'; the current color loads on page load; using the color picker or preset buttons updates the displayed text color immediately and persists via POST so reload shows the updated color."
    },
    {
      "id": "testing-and-readme",
      "title": "Document usage and perform manual tests",
      "description": "Write a README with setup, build, and run instructions and include manual test steps (start server, open UI, change color, reload). Verify all endpoints and UI interactions work as expected.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project",
        "install-dependencies",
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-express-server",
        "implement-persistence",
        "build-frontend-ui"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "README contains clear run/build/test steps; following them results in a working app where color changes persist and UI updates correctly."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down functional decomposition: set up project and dependencies, configure styling, implement backend APIs and persistence, build the frontend UI that interacts with the API, then document and test."
}
